title: April 2024
excerpt: Learning Java and the beginning of the journey.
content:
My plan at the time was to become very good in my profession: learn an amazing field, get better jobs, and potentially build something of my own.

I started learning Java to follow that path.

title: October 2024
excerpt: Building a chess engine and wanting projects that tell a story.
content:
After I learned the fundamentals in Java, and after exploring a Java framework at work, I decided it's time to make a serious project. Build up a portfolio.

Since I enjoy chess, I started building a chess engine.

In the following months, I spent some time after work on it and I began to find the project more and more interesting. I was increasingly convinced that from now on I want to work on projects like this one. Projects that are interesting but at the same time more personal, and can describe a part of my personality, like enjoying chess.

title: February 2025
excerpt: Turning the chess engine into a full chess web application.
content:
If a project tells a part of my personality, I want people to experience it the way I communicate it.

I decided to turn the chess engine, previously run locally, into a full chess web application, SKGCHESS.

I spend the next months building it with great enthusiasm.

title: April 2025
excerpt: First move over websockets, UI, and learning that appearance and story matter.
content:
For the first time, I had a move sent over WebSockets to the other player and displayed in the frontend. I was very happy to see it working, and next I wanted to shape its appearance to communicate and express myself.

This part took much longer than I expected. I wanted visiting the site to create a specific atmosphere, almost like a small narrative, expressed through the content, the colors, the music, the quotes, and the overall presentation.

I realized that I am more interested in the user experiencing what I create as a narrative than in the functionality of the application.

Moreover, I understood that most professional software exists to serve a clear purpose and ultimately create profit.

This made me reflect on my work as a software engineer.

Although I am very proud of it, respect it as a field, and I will always enjoy working with people who share the same passion for software, specifically people who are collaborative and committed to producing exceptional work, I realized that I have a need to create things that have meaning beyond myself. Things that are created with the purpose of touching on broader ideas, raising questions about social or human matters, and inviting reflection, rather than being constrained to meet expectations and just work as expected.

I continued refining SKGCHESS to match the way I had imagined it, until finally stepping away from it in June.

title: June 2025
excerpt: Two paths (game development or freelancing), first game concept, and the chess club email.
content:
Having understood that SKGCHESS is a personal project that attempted to tell a story to those who visited it, I started thinking in ways that I could continue viewing my work from this perspective, but creating something that people would want to be more invested in and actively engaged, much like we do when commiting to watch a movie or play a game.

I had two ways:

  • Game development
  • Freelancing (I was thinking of trying out indie projects where I can express my aesthetics. I was still troubled though with what type of project could achieve that).

I decided to try game development, but since I know how hard and time-consuming it is, I first wanted to make sure it was the right choice.

NOTE: Before June 2025, I had already tried to play with a game engine, the first time being in winter in 2023, where I just messed up with things, and the second in February of 2025 while developing SKGCHESS, where I spend a few days modeling on Blender. It's worth noting that the model of the dog appearing in the front page, was made during those days. It is my dog Lilith, as you can see here :)

(image of lilith)

This time, since I wanted to validate the reasons behind choosing to make a game and not explore if I would enjoy making one, I did not open up any engine. Instead, I tried to find out if I have a story to tell and indeed need to create.

So I wrote down an idea for a scenario. Very roughly: you are inside the mind of a protagonist who is in a state of dormancy, and you are asked to explore its different regions, unlock their lost emotions and wake them up from numbness.

Afterwards, I started thinking about gameplay mechanics around this idea. For example, the player could have a weapon that fires emotions, and depending on the emotion it shoots, it has different effects on the environment. There are many more ideas, but it's probably better not to expand too much here.

I found myself being more attracted, though, to the atmosphere and feeling of the game. Specifically, on analyzing how I could create something cinematic, thought-provoking, handling existential questions, or philosophizing, while also being fun to play.

I was happy with what I had and for the next step to explore game development, I decided it would be wise to better understand what a game designer actually does. So I read a book about game design named "A Theory of Fun for Game Design" by Raph Koster. I took away many interesting insights from the book and continued thinking through what I want to create, trying to be sure before committing to make a game.

End June 2025

I received an email about SKGCHESS. I had posted it on social media some time ago, and a friend from work approached me to help.

The whole discussion pushed me to return to the project again and refine it further before posting it on the website of one of the few local chess clubs in my hometown, Thessaloniki.

title: August 2025
excerpt: Launching IXLSTUDIO as a freelancing page.
content:
SKGCHESS was almost ready to be shared with the local chess club. This whole effort also pushed me to think about starting freelancing, which seemed like a more realistic and immediate path compared to creating a game.

I launched IXLSTUDIO as a small studio page where I could present my work and potentially offer services. The idea was that people who discovered SKGCHESS could also visit IXLSTUDIO and see the broader direction of what I was creating.

During the rest of the month, I spent time experimenting with the design of IXLSTUDIO, trying to shape how this space would represent both my technical work and the ideas behind it.

title: September 2025
excerpt: Taking a step back, Unreal Engine, painting, Blender, FL Studio, and the big questions.
content:
After working on it a little more and having everything ready, I went on vacation and had some time to reflect.

Coming back, I decided to postpone the launch.

I had spent more than 1 year in what felt like constant exploration, and I decided I needed to slow down a bit.

title: November 2025
excerpt: T a step back, Unreal Engine, painting, Blender, FL Studio, and the big questions.
content:
I returned to the idea of game development, but this time in a calmer way.

Since I had already explored the story I wanted to tell, understood what a game designer actually does, and experimented with Blender earlier, my next question was whether the game should be 3D or 2D.

To answer this, I needed to actually work in a game engine and experience what each approach had to offer.

I followed a tutorial in Unreal Engine. Since my PC could not run the engine smoothly, I rented the Shadow PC service. Over the next few days, I created some landscapes and experimented with assets. It was genuinely creative and engaging.

This sparked a broader curiosity about art in general. If I were to make a game, I wanted to be able to create my own art in some way.

The question of 2D versus 3D became more pressing with art in mind. Essentially, I was trying to figure out whether I could achieve what I imagined without using 3D, since although I prefer 3D, I know how difficult it can be.

Along the way, I began painting for the first time and read a book gifted to me by a good friend, The Story of Art by Gombrich. I also started experimenting with Blender and FL Studio for music.

Over the following months, I engaged with these activities from time to time, and I frequently returned to reevaluate the game development idea.

title: January 2026
excerpt: Writing the complete story and the world document.
content:
After some time, I now knew I wanted to create and was committed to pursuing it.

Now, to investigate the game development idea seriously, I came back to what I did in June with the scenario attempt and this time I tried harder. I wrote a complete story, a mixed version of my first scenario, along with new direction. I made a document with the entire history of the world: how it could be delivered narratively, what type of game could help achieve the message, and how the gameplay mechanics could connect with the story of the world.

title: February 2026
excerpt: Switching to open source and looking into Godot.
content: After moving away from Spotify, I decided more broadly that I want to avoid proprietary software. I switched fully to open source. I reinstalled Linux (which I had used as a student), purged Windows, and with it the Shadow PC service as well. With this shift, I decided that if I were to make a game, it should also be in an open source engine. So I started looking into Godot Engine.

title: March 2026
excerpt: Starting the game in Godot and Day 1 with the documentation.
content:
The journey of almost 1.5 years of constant searching feels like it is finally coming to an end. Along the way, I had many questions and explored finding meaning in many areas. I changed my perspective on things multiple times. I imagine that every few years in a person's life, something like this happens: a new perspective emerges, and a new chapter begins.

A few days ago, I finished ixlstudio in the form you see now. At the same time, I also started teaching some classes related to my profession at a college. My daily job continues to go well.

All the work is coming together.

I will make a game in Godot, and in 2D.

I will try to create my own art.

This is project make a game.

Go to the journal to start following the documentation.
